main_sections.title: Spectacular Wonders
main_sections.prose_summary:
'Spectacular Wonders' is an informational text that describes seven remarkable natural phenomena of India, celebrating the country's extraordinary and diverse landscapes. The text opens with a broad statement about India's mystifying natural phenomena and then profiles each wonder individually.

The Valley of Flowers in Chamoli, Uttarakhand is a UNESCO World Heritage Site containing around 600 exotic flower species, rare wildlife including the snow leopard, and spectacular Himalayan backdrops. The Living Root Bridges of Meghalaya are engineering feats built by indigenous people using intertwined roots of trees, solving the age-old problem of crossing waterways during monsoons; some bridges are 175 feet long and can persist for hundreds of years. The Lonar Crater Lake in Buldhana, Maharashtra, was formed by a meteorite impact thousands of years ago and is the only crater in India formed in basaltic rock, ranking third largest globally; the crater holds a unique saline lake. The Magnetic Hills near Leh, Ladakh create an optical illusion where vehicles appear to roll uphill on the so-called Magnetic Road, caused by the peculiar alignment of surrounding slopes and the horizon. The Glowing Waters of Kerala at Kumbalangi village near Kochi exhibit bioluminescence caused by a high concentration of micro-planktons, making this an eco-tourism destination. The Sundarbans in West Bengal, spread over 1,400 km in the delta of the Ganga, Brahmaputra, and Meghna rivers, is a mangrove forest home to Bengal tigers, estuarine crocodiles, Indian pythons, and diverse bird species. Finally, Chandipur Beach in Balasore, Odisha is known for the sea retreating 2–5 km during ebb tide and returning with high tide, creating the illusion of the sea playing hide-and-seek with visitors.

The text concludes by framing these phenomena as a blend of scientific intrigue and folklore magic, reminding readers of the extraordinary forces shaping our environment.

reading_for_comprehension.section_name: Let us read + Let us discuss + Let us think and reflect

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Do these activities before we read: I. Look at the pictures and sort them into natural and man-made wonders. II. Which of these wonders—man-made or natural—are more appealing and why? Share your answers. III. Write down two wonders of nature that have amazed you. Discuss what makes them impressive. IV. Work in groups of four. Complete the web chart with words you associate with the phrase 'Nature's Power'.
Complete the information in the table given below. One example has been done for you.

question_bank.stem: Complete the table of Amazing Natural Phenomena, Location, and One Unique Feature. (1. Valley of Flowers — Chamoli, Uttarakhand — Around 600 species of exotic flowers [example given]. Fill in for: 2. The Living Root Bridges / 3. Lonar Crater Lake — Buldhana, Maharashtra / 4. Magnetic Hills / 5. Glowing Waters / 6. Sundarbans — West Bengal / 7. Chandipur Beach — Balasore, Odisha.)
question_bank.table:
Amazing Natural Phenomena|Location|One Unique Feature
The Valley of Flowers|Chamoli district, Uttarakhand|Around 600 species of exotic flowers
The Living Root Bridges|__|__
Lonar Crater Lake|Buldhana district, Maharashtra|__
Magnetic Hills|__|__
Glowing Waters|__|__
The Sundarbans|West Bengal|__
Chandipur Beach|Balasore district, Odisha|__

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the given extracts and answer the questions that follow. Extract 1: 'India is home to some of the most mystifying natural phenomena that surprise and spark our imagination.'

question_bank.stem: Replace the underlined phrase with one from the extract. India is the birthplace of several rare species of flora and fauna that thrive in its diverse ecosystems.
What does the phrase 'nature's unusual side' suggest about the phenomena described?

Complete the similarity by choosing the correct option. spark : imagination :: ______________ : ______________. A. inspiration; creativity  B. wonder; amazement  C. effort; reward  D. knowledge; books
Identify whether the following statement is true or false. Some natural phenomena can surprise people.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the given extract and answer the questions. Extract 2: 'Have you seen things disappearing in front of your eyes?…This happens in Odisha. It is a beach in the Balasore district where one can witness the sea disappearing mysteriously—the Chandipur Beach which is known for its unique feature of playing hide-and-seek with its visitors.'

question_bank.stem: Complete the following sentence: The writer's most likely purpose to introduce information about the Chandipur Beach phenomenon with a question is to ____________________. (engage the reader's curiosity and create a sense of suspense / draw the reader to learn more about the unusual phenomenon / set the tone for the surprising nature of the content that follows)
options: engage the reader's curiosity and create a sense of suspense
draw the reader to learn more about the unusual phenomenon
set the tone for the surprising nature of the content that follows

question_bank.stem: Select the option that is correct for both Assertion (A) and Reason (R). (A): Chandipur Beach in Odisha is known for its unique feature of the sea disappearing. (R): The tides retreat drastically, exposing the Chandipur beach. A. Both (A) and (R) are true, and (R) is the correct explanation of (A). B. Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A). C. (A) is true, but (R) is false. D. (A) is false, but (R) is true.
What does the term 'unique' suggest about the beach in Balasore? A. very popular  B. one of its kind  C. ancient place  D. far from city
What does the writer want to convey with the use of the phrase 'playing hide-and-seek with its visitors'?
How are the living root bridges a boon for the local people of Meghalaya?
Why is the Lonar Crater Lake considered a remarkable and rare site?
What might be the writer's purpose of highlighting the awe-inspiring natural wonders of India?
The writer uses descriptive language and introductory questions for some of the natural phenomena. What impact does it have on the readers?
Which of these unusual phenomena did you find the most interesting and why?

listening.transcript_text:
Brother: I liked the pictures Uncle Dileep posted yesterday from the Republic of Peru. Can you guess which was my favourite?
Sister: I know it was the Rainbow Mountain. I say that because it was certainly my favourite.
Brother: Really! I didn't know it was your favourite too. Can you imagine the creativity in Nature? What a marvellous natural phenomenon!
Sister: I could not believe my eyes when I saw that picture. There are so many colours there. Do you know how it's formed?
Brother: Of course! It's actually quite amazing. The mountain gets its colours from layers of minerals like clay, limestone, and sandstone that got deposited millions of years ago.
Sister: Wow, I read that too. I think each colour is due to a different mineral. Like, the green comes from copper, and the red from clay. It's like nature painted the mountain!
Brother: I cannot believe the fact that it was all buried under snow until recently.
Sister: Oh! The melting snow must have made it visible again. That's why it looks so fresh and bright today.
Brother: Those layers of minerals were actually deposited by water from ancient seas and lakes.
Sister: Each colour represents a different time in history. That's why it's called the rainbow mountain.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You will listen to a brother and sister discuss a natural phenomenon. As you listen, mark the four true statements from 1–6 given below.

question_bank.stem: Mark true or false: 1. The brother was taken aback to see the posted pictures. 2. The brother admitted to not knowing about his sister's favourite picture. 3. The sister stated that the rainbow mountain was like any other mountain. 4. The brother was aware of the reason for the formation of colours on the mountain. 5. The brother was surprised to learn about the mountain being covered with snow till recently. 6. The sister understood nature's role in the beauty of the mountain.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Work in groups of four. Make a presentation on a place of interest in and around your region that people like to visit. Guidelines: Slides 1–2: choose a popular place. Slides 3–4: location and how to reach. Slides 5–6: attractions, popularity, historical importance. Slides 7–8: weather, local food, customs, language. Slides 9–10: best time to visit and reason for recommendation.

Write a descriptive essay for your school magazine describing anything from nature that attracted your attention. It may be an object, a place, or an event experienced by you. Remember to give a title. Use proper format and content organisation—begin with a title, include introduction (one paragraph), body (two to three paragraphs), and conclusion (one paragraph).
Fill in the blanks with the correct expressions from the text given in the box: came into existence / to no end / in full glory / in disbelief / to name a few / age-old problem.

question_bank.stem: Fill in: 1. I have read many stories _____. 2. Do you know when this little town _____? 3. His hard work has helped him _____. 4. There are no simple solutions to the _____ of traffic jams. 5. He opened the ancient wooden box and stared at the treasure _____. 6. The garden is _____ in spring with lots of beautiful flowers in bloom.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Select the correct synonyms from the box that match the list of words 1–5. You may refer to a thesaurus. Box: colossal / exotic / serene / enigmatic / spectacular.

question_bank.stem: Match synonyms: 1. mysterious, puzzling, baffling / 2. mighty, gigantic, huge / 3. breath-taking, stunning, awe-inspiring / 4. peaceful, calm, tranquil / 5. unusual, peculiar, foreign.
question_bank.table:
Description|Word
mysterious, puzzling, baffling|__
mighty, gigantic, huge|__
breath-taking, stunning, awe-inspiring|__
peaceful, calm, tranquil|__
unusual, peculiar, foreign|__

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Binomials are a pair of fixed words joined by 'and' or 'or'. Match the binomials in Column 1 with their meanings in Column 2. Then use the binomials in sentences of your own.

question_bank.stem: Match: 1. odds and ends / 2. on and off / 3. mix and match / 4. all or nothing / 5. part and parcel / 6. pick and choose / 7. sooner or later / 8. leaps and bounds — with meanings: (i) put different things together / (ii) complete part of or belong to / (iii) choose only the best / (iv) at some time in the future / (v) sometimes, occasionally / (vi) increase or develop very quickly / (vii) small, unimportant things / (viii) something done completely or not at all.
question_bank.table:
Binomial|Meaning
odds and ends|(i) put different things together to get a range of possibilities
on and off|(ii) complete part of or belong to
mix and match|(iii) choose only the best (things, people, etc.)
all or nothing|(iv) at some time in the future
part and parcel|(v) sometimes, occasionally
pick and choose|(vi) increase or develop very quickly
sooner or later|(vii) small, unimportant things
leaps and bounds|(viii) something to be done completely or not at all

question_bank.stem: Use the binomials from Column 1 in sentences of your own.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Study the highlighted words in sentences from the text ('living', 'striking', 'rugged') — these are participles (verb forms used as adjectives). Present participles end in -ing and describe ongoing actions; past participles usually end in -ed/-d/-t/-en/-n and describe completed actions. Fill in the blanks using present participle or past participle form of the verbs in the box: stand / amaze / carve / scatter / glow.

question_bank.stem: Fill in blanks with correct participle form: 1. The _______________ marble monument looks even more spectacular in the moonlight. (carve) 2. The _______________ view of the Himalayas from Darjeeling attracts visitors. (amaze) 3. The Sun Temple in Konark features intricately _______________ stone wheels and walls. (carve) 4. _______________ at a height of 182 m, the Statue of Unity is dedicated to Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel. (stand) 5. The _______________ islands of the Andaman and Nicobar offer some of the cleanest beaches. (scatter)

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Study the highlighted word 'Crossing' in the sentence from the text — this is a gerund (verb form used as a noun, ending in -ing). Gerunds can serve as subject, complement, object of a verb, object of a preposition, or after certain verbs. Underline the gerunds and participles in the following sentences. Write 'G' for gerunds and 'P' for participles.

question_bank.stem: Identify gerund (G) or participle (P): 1. Dancing is a great way to express emotions. 2. Travelling can broaden your perspective. 3. The falling leaves signalled the arrival of autumn. 4. She enjoys cooking new recipes for her family. 5. Reading helps improve your vocabulary. 6. He watched the shimmering stars in the night sky. 7. Forgetting the keys inside, Kanchan locked the door.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Combine the following pairs of sentences using participles or gerunds. (Example: 'Entering the room, Nitesh saw his friend sitting in the corner.')

question_bank.stem: 2. Smitha walked carelessly. She slipped on the doormat. (participle)
3. Sleep for eight hours. It is good for health. (gerund)
4. Ranjith felt happy about the result. He called his parents. (participle)
5. Father encouraged Meena. Meena joined the course. (gerund)

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Man-made Wonders: 1. Read about Kallanai Dam in Tamil Nadu — one of the world's oldest dams, built by the Chola King Karikala around 150 CE. 2. Read about Nalanda in Bihar — the world's first residential university, declared UNESCO heritage site in 1980. Visit https://nalandauniv.edu.in/about-nalanda/history-and-revival/ 3. Read about Hampi in Karnataka — Vittala Temple's 56 SaReGaMa pillars that produce musical notes when tapped. 4. Modern wonders: Bandra-Worli Sea Link, Statue of Unity, Bhupen Hazarika Setu, and others.

Some micro-planktons in the sea are bioluminescent. Have you seen any other life form that has this quality? Identify the insect given below and find out what it is called in your language. Look out for it when you visit a field or a place with a wetland.
